vitals.participation_trend: Popular early-season event, places fill quickly

course_description.overview: Club-run sportive with long (80 miles / 125 km) and short (60 miles / 95 km) circular routes through Surrey countryside, Guildford and Woking area, starting from Downside Village Hall. Fully signposted and marshalled, electronically timed.[1][2]

key_segments.name: Surrey Hills AONB
key_segments.description: Challenging terrain tackling Box Hill and Leith Hill from the 2012 Olympic road course.

key_segments.name: Feedstops
key_segments.description: Two on long route, one on short route with toilets.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Early-season challenging sportive through hilly Surrey Hills with significant elevation gain, popular for friendly atmosphere among local riders.

logistics.getting_there: Start/finish at Downside Village Hall entrance (Cobham area). Park at Holly Hedge Car Park KT11 3DQ (5-min ride). Sign-on 07:30-09:00, departures 07:30-10:00.[2]
logistics.registration_details: £22-£30. Entries open early, places fill quickly. Race numbers and timing chips at registration.[1][2]
logistics.bike_logistics: Road bikes suitable. Toilets at HQ and feedstops; coffee/cake available at HQ.[2]
logistics.local_considerations: Early season event, March weather variable. Staffed by Twickenham Cycling Club members (except First Aid). Finish by 15:00.[1][2]

final_verdict.one_liner: Friendly early-season road sportive with hilly Surrey classics at bargain price.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Local UK road cyclists seeking challenging hills, Olympic routes, and club-run vibe without big crowds.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for value, atmosphere, and early-season fitness test.
pro_tips: Arrive early for parking 5 mins from HQ.
Train for 2400m+ climbing on Box/Leith Hills.
Layer for March UK weather variability.
